old_claim.note: Yokes & Harnesses provides traction arrangements for animal-powered treading or threshing sledges; heavy draft plowing is a related field use rather than a direct prerequisite.

new_claim_absent: No yoke or harness dependency remains because the Raemkai scene shows donkeys directly treading grain and does not depict an animal-drawn board or traction implement.

source_shape.source_locator: Bottom Register describes donkeys being driven to trample the barley threshing floor; no sledge, board, yoke, or harness is part of the described operation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The documented animal-powered method is hoof treading rather than pulling a separate threshing machine.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A traction-system edge would import the mechanics of a different threshing implementation into a scene whose animals contact the crop directly.

ontology_before: Direct animal treading and animal-drawn threshing boards were merged into one harness-dependent method.
ontology_after: The Raemkai node models only the directly depicted unyoked donkey-treading implementation.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: animal_powered_threshing no longer directly depends on yokes_and_harnesses.
would_reject_if: Detailed examination of the Raemkai wall established that the donkeys pull a yoked threshing implement hidden or omitted from the current catalog description.
short_reason: The donkeys tread the grain directly and do not pull a depicted implement.
